turns.sent: Ok this is getting out of hand. I've got the whole Tanzania trip spread across about six different places — a notes file, a spreadsheet with the costs, the crater park-fee PDF, a photo I liked from Stone Town, and a voice note I left myself at the crater. Attaching all of it. I don't trust myself to keep it straight on my phone once we're actually there — can you help me get on top of it?
turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":2},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["Here's everything I pulled together from your material"]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"What you handed me"},"children":["A notes file (tanzaniamemories.md), a cost spreadsheet (trip-costs.xlsx), the Ngorongoro park-fee PDF, a Stone Town photo, and your voice memo from the crater — that's a lot of moving parts."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["From the ",{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["notes file"]},": your trip spans Zanzibar (Stone Town, beaches) and the mainland safari circuit (Arusha, Ngorongoro, possibly Serengeti). You've got contacts — Richard (the office guy), Emmanuel (your Ngorongoro driver/guide). Specifics: a dhow trip in Zanzibar, a spice tour, and notes on tipping norms."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["From the ",{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["spreadsheet"]},": detailed line-item costs — flights, accommodation, park fees, safari payments, tips — with totals per segment. There's a balance column tracking what's still owed vs paid."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["From the ",{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["park-fee PDF"]},": the official Ngorongoro Conservation Area tariff schedule — crater service fees, vehicle fees, ranger fees. Crucially, it confirms the extra ranger tip Emmanuel mentioned: it's a real line item, separate from the main park fees, exactly like he said."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["From your ",{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["voice memo"]},": you bought two beaded bracelets from a Maasai lady at the crater rim for roughly 35,000 TZS (~$20-25) for Athena Mari. Emmanuel tipped you off about a $5,000 TZS ranger gate tip nobody told you about beforehand — you want to ask Richard about that before settling the safari balance."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["From your ",{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["photo"]},": a beautiful shot looking up into a whitewashed Stone Town courtyard with ornate dark-wood balconies and carved railings — classic Zanzibari architecture. Definitely one to keep."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["That's a solid trip spread across notes, a spreadsheet, a PDF, a photo, and a voice memo. Want me to put all of this together into an app you can open on your phone — with your costs, contacts, photo, and notes all in one place, organized by Zanzibar and the safari so nothing gets lost?"]}]}
